summary.intent: Address third-party code review (Codex) findings on PR #674 in fogodev/ars-ui, specifically fixing DropZone state machine gaps around file drag hover types, SetProps validity recomputation, and extension accept tokens.
summary.outcome: All three Codex findings were patched with regression tests; local validation (unit tests, spec conformance, proptest, clippy, coverage) passed green; commit pushed to PR #674 with co-author trailer.

repo: DropZone state machine lives in crates/ars-components/src/utility/drop_zone/mod.rs and has a corresponding spec at spec/components/utility/drop-zone.md that must be kept in sync.
Proptest state machine tests for DropZone are in crates/ars-components/tests/proptest_state_machines/utility.rs and run under --ignored flag.
The repo uses cargo xtask spec validate to cross-check spec conformance, separate from cargo test spec_conformance.
Browser file drag events advertise 'Files' as the MIME type, not individual file extensions — the machine must treat 'Files' as a potentially valid hover and defer extension matching to drop time.
SetProps must trigger validity recomputation against remembered hover types, requiring the machine to store advertised drag types as internal context state.
Codex reviews are triggered via PR comment '@codex review' and signal completion via GitHub reactions on the issue.

code.finding: Context struct needed a new field to store the current advertised hover drag types so SetProps can recompute validity without a new drag event.
DragEnter/DragOver handling needed to treat 'Files' as a valid potential type and store advertised types for later revalidation.
Drop validation for extension accept tokens must match against actual file names at drop time, not hover time.
Proptest inputs and state machine model needed updating to cover the new SetProps revalidation path and Files drag type.

workflow: Codex review status is tracked via GitHub reactions (in-progress reaction = still running) and unresolved threads; polling both together is the reliable signal.
The pattern for a review iteration is: reply to threads with fix commit reference → resolve threads → trigger '@codex review' → poll reactions+threads until no unresolved and reaction changes.
Local validation order: fmt → lib tests → spec conformance → spec validate → proptest (--ignored) → clippy → coverage, before pushing.
git commit co-author trailer 'Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>' is required for review-fix commits in this repo.

friction: Long polling intervals (60s, 120s, 180s, 240s) were needed to wait for Codex's review reaction to change, with no push-based notification available.
CI had several jobs still pending (unit tests, coverage, release verification) during the review wait, making it hard to distinguish 'review stalled' from 'CI still running'.
Cargo build locks caused spec conformance and spec validation commands to queue behind other cargo invocations, adding latency to the validation loop.
Codex's intermediate in-progress state with no unresolved threads created ambiguity about whether the review had silently passed or was still processing.
open_items: Remote CI coverage and release verification jobs were still pending when the session ended — final CI green status not confirmed.
The Codex third-pass verdict had not yet produced a final approval reaction at session end; a fourth poll or human review may be needed to confirm merge readiness.
No snapshot test updates were mentioned for the DropZone changes — worth confirming snapshots are not stale after the Files drag and extension matching changes.
